criteria.name: Cites specific evidence
criteria.description: References measurable metrics, named data sources, or specific statistics — not vague claims

criteria.name: Names builders/actors
criteria.description: Identifies specific people, organizations, or institutions actively working on this challenge

criteria.name: Accuracy
criteria.description: Claims are factually correct and appropriately sourced (no hallucinated statistics)

criteria.name: Recency
criteria.description: Uses current data, acknowledges what's changed recently, doesn't rely on outdated figures

criteria.name: Acknowledges complexity
criteria.description: Presents progress without flattening — notes reversals, regional variations, or contested data

criteria.name: Applies analytical framework
criteria.description: Uses a structured lens (exponential curves, conditions/obstacles, technology maturity staging) — not just listing facts

criteria.name: Names shadow/risk honestly
criteria.description: Identifies who gets left behind, transition costs, distortion risks, or failure modes without undermining the core thesis

criteria.name: States conditions explicitly
criteria.description: Names specific conditions under which the positive trajectory holds AND conditions under which it fails

criteria.name: Maps connections
criteria.description: Identifies how this domain relates to other domains — downstream effects, dependencies, or tensions

criteria.name: Holds the middle
criteria.description: Avoids both hype (everything is great) and cynicism (nothing works) — calibrated confidence

criteria.name: Frames as solvable
criteria.description: Presents the challenge as a construction project, not a fate — agency, not helplessness

criteria.name: Identifies leverage points
criteria.description: Names specific technologies, approaches, policies, or organizations as highest-impact entry points

criteria.name: Suggests concrete first steps
criteria.description: Gives actionable next moves for the specific persona in the prompt, not generic advice

criteria.name: Connects to real examples
criteria.description: References people, organizations, or communities actually doing this successfully

criteria.name: Empowers without overwhelming
criteria.description: Makes the reader feel capable and motivated, not guilty, paralyzed, or lectured
